sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 44

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 44:1
items.html: (to the question) As long as the embryo receives the Lights of Nefesh — meaning from below upward — it is fit to receive also the discernment of GAR, which are the Lights of Yechida, Haya, Neshama, since they are received in the discernment of Nefesh. But when it is completed in all the discernments of the Ibur [conception], it awakens to receive its Lights from above downward; and since the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] is still present in its Eynaim [eyes], the Lights would be passing below the Tzimtzum [restriction]. Therefore the opening of the navel was sealed, and the door and hinge of Katnut awaken to their dominion — that is, she again awakens the boundary of the Hey Tachtona in the Eynaim that is present in that hinge — and with this it parts and goes out from the joining with the door and hinge of Gadlut, for in the door and hinge of Gadlut there is no Hey Tachtona in the Eynaim. And since the doors are now not of one discernment in the closed Mem, they open and draw apart from one another, and the newborn comes out into the air of the world. And remember here that spiritual remoteness is disparity of form. Therefore, when the Nukva received the door and hinge of Gadlut from Ima — namely, when the Hey Tachtona descended from her Eynaim to the Peh, and the AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] returned to her — then, although all the discernments of Katnut remain in her, from the principle that there is no absence in the spiritual, so that there also remains in her the discernment of the door and hinge of Katnut, which has the Hey Tachtona in the Eynaim — nevertheless it stands there without any dominion or striking at all. Hence the two doors — of Katnut and of Gadlut — are both actually one discernment, with no Hey Tachtona in their Eynaim, and so they are joined together in a closed Mem as one discernment. But when the Nukva herself again awakens the measure of Katnut in her, for the protection of the newborn, so that it will not be able to receive from the Lights of Gadlut, as above — whereby the door and hinge of Katnut renew the dominion they had at the time of the Nukva's Katnut — a remote disparity of form is immediately made between the door and hinge of Gadlut, in which there is no force of Tzimtzum at all, and the door and hinge of Katnut, to which the force of Tzimtzum returned from the Hey Tachtona. These doors are thus found far removed from one another, and the doors open wide, and the newborn can now come out into the air of the world. Understand this. (p. 791, beginning "However"; p. 795, beginning "And this")
